calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion Of Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Lyon of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for Plaintiff Fong & Chan Architects.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT WASHINGTON HOSPITAL HEALTHCARE SYSTEM'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is denied. See the court's ruling on the petition to confirm the arbitration award. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Fong And Chan Architects Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Lyon of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for Plaintiff Fong & Chan Architects.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT WASHINGTON HOSPITAL HEALTHCARE SYSTEM'S Motion To Dismiss Fong And Chan Architects Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is rendered moot in light of the court's rulings on the petition to confirm the arbitration award and the petition to vacate the arbitration award. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Lyon of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for Plaintiff Fong & Chan Architects.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER FONG & CHAN ARCHITECTS Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award - The court grants the petition to confirm the arbitration award and the court denies the petition to vacate the arbitration award. The 2002 Standard Form and the 2008 Addendum were agreements between Plaintiff, the architect, and Defendant, the client. The 2002 Standard Form and the 2008 Addendum met the requirements for a valid written contract between an architect and a client. (Business and Professions Code section 5536.22; (Lyon Exhibit 2 Articles 3, 7, 10.) The plain language of the code section does not require the contract to identify specifically the price terms, the name of the job, or the work which is to be performed for a particular job. Furthermore, it is disingenuous for Defendant to argue that the 2002 Standard Form and the 2008 Addendum did not pertain to the Pavilion Project when in its cross-complaint Defendant identified the 2002 Standard Form and the 2008 Addendum as the contractual bases for Plaintiff's work on the Pavilion Project. Defendant's claim that it attempted to amend the cross-complaint and that it argued in front of the arbitration panel that the two documents were not the contractual bases for Plaintiff's work on the Pavilion Project is unpersuasive. Additionally, the award issued by the arbitration panel was appropriate and valid. Under AAA Construction Arbitration Rule 48, the arbitrator may fashion any remedy that is just and equitable. The termination fee awarded by the arbitration panel bore a rational relationship to the breach of the underlying contract because the award is what the parties bargained for in the event of the contract's termination. (Lyon Exhibit 2 Article10 ; Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. v. Intel Corp. (1994) 9 Cal.4th 362, 367 (An award remedy may be set aside where the remedy granted bears no rational relationship to the breach of the underlying contract).) The panel's conclusion that the award was valid per Civil Code section 1671, based on Defendant's failure to demonstrate that the award was unreasonable, was a valid exercise of the arbitration panel's power. Because the arbitration award was based on an enforceable agreement, which contained an enforceable arbitration clause and because the award issued by the arbitration panel was appropriate and a valid exercise of the arbitration panel's powers under AAA Construction Arbitration Rule 48, the court grants the petition to confirm the arbitration award and the court denies Defendant's petition to vacate the arbitration award. The prevailing is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Dec-17-2015 Continued To Jan-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

PETITIONER FONG & CHAN ARCHITECTS' Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is continued to January 5, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 on the Court's own motion. Matter to be heard with Defendant's Motion of Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/HK)
